themes.label: Claude Code vs Cursor: The battle for the agentic IDE heats up with new Cursor 3 release.
Claude Opus 4.7 sets a new ceiling for reasoning and long-context performance.
Managed agent toolkits from Anthropic signal shift toward production-grade agent infrastructure.

artifacts.title: The debugging wars: Cursor 3 takes aim at Claude Code’s agentic edge
artifacts.desc: Analysis of the latest agent features in Cursor 3 vs Claude Code, specifically around terminal-native vs editor-native debugging.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Daniel uses Claude Code; Cursor 3 is a direct competitor for his agentic coding workflow.

artifacts.title: awesome-ai-agents-2026
artifacts.desc: Curated list of frameworks and tools for the 2026 agent ecosystem.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Central reference for his AI mastery project and Globe & Atlas content.

artifacts.title: Claude Opus 4.7 Released
artifacts.desc: Significant upgrade to the Opus model family with massive performance gains in coding and reasoning.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Primary model family in Daniel's stack.

artifacts.title: claude-managed-agents
artifacts.desc: Anthropic's official toolkit for scaling agents in production.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Official infra for his primary LLM provider.

artifacts.title: Maths vs machine learning publishing venues [D]
artifacts.desc: Academic debate on research publishing trends in 2026.

exercises.title: Benchmark Claude Code vs Cursor 3 on a complex GIS script
exercises.desc: Use both tools to refactor a legacy FME Python script. Compare speed, context accuracy, and 'instinct' for geospatial libraries.

exercises.title: Migrate your main agentic workflow to Claude Opus 4.7
exercises.desc: Update your environment variables and test the new reasoning capabilities on your most complex directives.
